summary: Tested IRS Offer Codes and 1095-C/1094-C form generation. All 15 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend tests passed. Critical fix verified: FT employees now correctly get offer codes 1B/1C/1D/1E (not 1H) when medical plans are available. 1095-C Line 14 codes match eligibility-calculated offer codes. IRS forms correctly use plan_library collection.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/enrollment/eligibility/{employer_id} returns 200 with 60 employees
Backend: All eligibility results have valid offer codes (1A-1H)
Backend: FT employees NOT assigned 1H when plans available (0 FT with 1H)
Backend: PT employees correctly get 1H (13 PT with 1H)
Backend: POST /api/enrollment/eligibility/run/{employer_id} recalculates offer codes correctly
Backend: Eligibility engine assigns correct codes - distribution: 1B(23), 1C(13), 1D(5), 1E(6), 1H(13)
Backend: GET /api/irs-forms/1095c/{employer_id}/2026 returns 47 forms
Backend: 1095-C Line 14 codes match eligibility offer codes
Backend: 1095-C forms have NO 1H or 1F codes for FT employees
Backend: GET /api/irs-forms/1094c/{employer_id}/2026 returns valid form
Backend: 1094-C summary uses plan_library (8 plans)
Backend: GET /api/irs-forms/summary/{employer_id}/2026 returns correct data
Backend: IRS summary plans_count matches plan_library (8 plans)
Backend: GET /api/enrollment/plans/{employer_id} returns 8 plans
Backend: POST /api/enrollment/plans/{plan_id}/compliance-check works correctly
Frontend: Login with fajju2001@gmail.com / test123 successful
Frontend: Dashboard loads with 60 employees, 47 FT, 57.25 FTE, ALE status
Frontend: IRS Forms page loads with summary cards
Frontend: Generate All Forms button works
Frontend: 1094-C tab shows transmittal form with employer info and monthly data
Frontend: 1095-C tab shows 47 forms with Line 14 codes (1B, 1C, 1D, 1E - no 1H)
Frontend: No error messages on any page
critical_code_review_comments: FPL threshold correctly set to $129.89 in irs_forms.py (line 66) for 2026
_recalculate_offer_code correctly falls back to all active medical plans when no specific plan_assignments exist
Decline handler correctly uses _recalculate_offer_code instead of hardcoding '1A'
All IRS form endpoints correctly use plan_library instead of legacy plans collection
1095-C Line 14 correctly overridden with pre-calculated eligibility offer codes
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing employer with 60 employees (47 FT, 13 PT), 8 plans in plan_library

mocked_apis_list: AI Analysis (rule-based fallback due to exhausted LLM budget)

context_for_next_testing_agent: IRS Offer Codes fully tested and working. Key findings: 1) Eligibility engine correctly assigns offer codes based on what is OFFERED (not enrolled). 2) FT employees get 1B/1C/1D/1E based on coverage tier offered, PT employees get 1H. 3) 1095-C Line 14 codes are overridden with pre-calculated eligibility offer codes. 4) All IRS form endpoints use plan_library collection (8 active plans). 5) 2026 rates verified: $129.89 FPL threshold, 9.96% affordability. Offer code distribution: 1B(23), 1C(13), 1D(5), 1E(6), 1H(13 PT only).

verified_fixes.eligibility_fallback: Falls back to all active medical plans when no plan_assignments exist
verified_fixes.irs_endpoints: All use plan_library instead of legacy plans collection
verified_fixes.line14_override: 1095-C Line 14 overridden with eligibility-calculated offer codes
